clear_gain_calibration.docs:
A button to clear the gain calibration from flash memory and use default values, or values defined under `gain_voltage` and `gain_ct`. All options from [Button](https://esphome.io/components/button/index.html#config-button).

*See also: [atm90e32 Button](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#button)*

clear_offset_calibration.docs:
A button to clear the gain calibration from flash memory and use default values, or values defined under `offset_voltage` and `offset_current`. All options from [Button](https://esphome.io/components/button/index.html#config-button).

*See also: [atm90e32 Button](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#button)*

clear_power_offset_calibration.docs:
A button to clear the gain calibration from flash memory and use default values, or values defined under `offset_active_power` and `offset_reactive_power`. All options from [Button](https://esphome.io/components/button/index.html#config-button).

*See also: [atm90e32 Button](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#button)*

id.docs:
**, [ID](https://esphome.io/guides/configuration-types.html#config-id)**: The ID of the `atm90e32` sensor defined above. Required if using more than one sensor.

*See also: [atm90e32 Button](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#button)*

run_gain_calibration.docs:
A button to run the gain calibration. `enable_gain_calibration` must be `True`. These values can be permanently stored in `gain_voltage` and `gain_ct`. All options from [Button](https://esphome.io/components/button/index.html#config-button).

*See also: [atm90e32 Button](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#button)*

run_offset_calibration.docs:
A button to run the offset calibration. `enable_offset_calibration` must be `True`. These values can be permanently stored in `offset_voltage` and `offset_current`. All options from [Button](https://esphome.io/components/button/index.html#config-button).

*See also: [atm90e32 Button](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#button)*

run_power_offset_calibration.docs:
A button to run the power offset calibration. `enable_offset_calibration` must be `True`. These values can be permanently stored in `offset_active_power` and `offset_reactive_power`. All options from [Button](https://esphome.io/components/button/index.html#config-button).

*See also: [atm90e32 Button](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#button)*

id.docs:
**, [ID](https://esphome.io/guides/configuration-types.html#config-id)**: The ID of the `atm90e32` sensor defined above. Required if using more than one sensor.

*See also: [atm90e32 Number](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#number)*

reference_current.docs:
Fine grained gain calibration for a known current of the circuit a CT is measuring. It is used to calculate the value for `gain_ct`. To be used in conjunction with the `run_gain_calibration` button. `enable_gain_calibration` must be `True`. Each phase below defaults to `min_value: 1`, `max_value: 200`, `mode: box`, `optimistic: True`, `step: 0.1`.
  * **phase_a** (*Optional*): Phase A reference current field. All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)
  
  * **phase_b** (*Optional*): Phase B reference current field. All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)
  
  * **phase_c** (*Optional*): Phase C reference current field. All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)
  
  

*See also: [atm90e32 Number](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#number)*

phase_a.docs:
Phase A reference current field. All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)

*See also: [atm90e32 Number](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#number)*

phase_b.docs:
Phase B reference current field. All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)

*See also: [atm90e32 Number](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#number)*

phase_c.docs:
Phase C reference current field. All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)

*See also: [atm90e32 Number](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#number)*

reference_voltage.docs:
Fine grained gain calibration of a known voltage that a voltage transformer is connected to. It is used to calculate the value for `gain_voltage`. To be used in conjunction with the `run_gain_calibration` button.  `enable_gain_calibration` must be `True`. Each phase below defaults to `min_value: 100`, `max_value: 200`, `mode: box`, `optimistic: True`, `step: 0.1`.
  * **phase_a** (*Optional*): Phase A reference voltage field. All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)
  
  * **phase_b** (*Optional*): Phase B reference voltage field. If not specified, will use the value from `phase_a` (CircuitSetup’s 6 channel meter has voltage phases tied together). All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)
  
  * **phase_c** (*Optional*): Phase C reference voltage field. If not specified, will use the value from `phase_a` (CircuitSetup’s 6 channel meter has voltage phases tied together). All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)
  
  

*See also: [atm90e32 Number](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#number)*

phase_a.docs:
Phase A reference voltage field. All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)

*See also: [atm90e32 Number](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#number)*

phase_b.docs:
Phase B reference voltage field. If not specified, will use the value from `phase_a` (CircuitSetup’s 6 channel meter has voltage phases tied together). All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)

*See also: [atm90e32 Number](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#number)*

phase_c.docs:
Phase C reference voltage field. If not specified, will use the value from `phase_a` (CircuitSetup’s 6 channel meter has voltage phases tied together). All options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number/index.html#config-number)

*See also: [atm90e32 Number](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#number)*

apparent_power.docs:
Use the apparent power value on this phase in VA. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

current.docs:
Use the current value of this phase in amperes. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

forward_active_energy.docs:
Use the forward active energy value on this phase in watt-hours. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

gain_ct.docs:
**, int**: CT clamp calibration value for this phase. Defaults to `27961`.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

gain_voltage.docs:
**, int**: Voltage gain value to scale the low voltage AC transformer to household mains feed. Defaults to `7305`.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

harmonic_power.docs:
Use the harmonic power value on this phase. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

offset_active_power.docs:
**, int**: The active power offset obtained using the power offset calibration feature when current is 0 and a voltage transformer is plugged in and calibrated. Defaults to `0`

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

offset_current.docs:
**, int**: The current offset obtained using the offset calibration feature when current and voltage are 0 Defaults to `0`

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

offset_reactive_power.docs:
**, int**: The reactive power offset obtained using the power offset calibration feature when current is 0 and a voltage transformer is plugged in and calibrated. Defaults to `0`

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

offset_voltage.docs:
**, int**: The voltage offset obtained using the offset calibration feature when current and voltage are 0 Defaults to `0`

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

peak_current.docs:
Use the peak current value on this phase in amperes. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

phase_angle.docs:
Use the phase angle value on this phase in degrees. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

power.docs:
Use the power value on this phase in watts. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

power_factor.docs:
Use the power factor value on this phase. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

reactive_power.docs:
Use the reactive power value on this phase in VAR. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

reverse_active_energy.docs:
Use the reverse active energy value on this phase in watt-hours. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

voltage.docs:
Use the voltage value of this phase in volts (RMS). All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

chip_temperature.docs:
Use the chip temperature value. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

cs_pin.docs:
**, [Pin Schema](https://esphome.io/guides/configuration-types.html#config-pin-schema)**: The pin CS is connected to. For the 6 channel meter main board, this will always be 5 and 4. For the add-on boards a jumper can be selected for each CS pin, but default to 0 and 16.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

current_phases.docs:
The number of phases the meter has, `2` or, `3` The 6 Channel Expandable Energy Meter should be set to `3`, and the Split Single Phase meter should be set to `2`. Defaults to `3`.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

enable_gain_calibration.docs:
**, boolean**: If true this enables gain calibration for voltage, `gain_voltage`, and current, `gain_ct`, based on the values entered in the corresponding voltage, `reference_voltage`, and current, `reference_current`,     reference fields. With multiple ATM90E32 sensors each one is enabled individually, and it’s buttons are mapped using an id value pair. e.g. `id: chip1` when more than one is defined. After running, the values are stored in chip memory and will be available upon reboot and OTA software updates.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

enable_offset_calibration.docs:
**, boolean**: If true this enables fine grained offset noise 0 level calibration for voltage, `offset_voltage`, and current sensors, `offset_current`, & power offset for active, `offset_active_power`, and reactive power, `offset_reactive_power`. Buttons are required to operate the calibration feature. With multiple ATM90E32 sensors each one is enabled individually, and it’s buttons are mapped using an id value pair. e.g. `id: chip1` when more than one is defined. After running,     the values are stored in chip memory and will be available upson reboot and OTA software updates. Offset calibration can only be performed when all voltage and current inputs are at a 0 value. USB power is recommended. For power offset calibration, only a voltage     transformer should be connected.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

frequency.docs:
Use the frequency value calculated by the meter. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

gain_pga.docs:
**, string**: Increase this when the output of the CT clamp is too low and `gain_ct` is maxed out. One of `1X`, `2X`, `4X`. Defaults to `1X`, which is suitable for most CT clamps.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

id.docs:
**, [ID](https://esphome.io/guides/configuration-types.html#config-id)**: Required if using more than one `atm90e32` chip with buttons for calibration, reference voltage/current fields, and phase status fields

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

line_frequency.docs:
**, string**: The AC line frequency of the supply voltage. One of `50Hz`, `60Hz`.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

peak_current_signed.docs:
**, boolean**: Control the peak current output as signed or absolute. Defaults to `false`.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

phase_a.docs:
The configuration options for the 1st phase.
  * **voltage** (*Optional*): Use the voltage value of this phase in volts (RMS). All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  * **current** (*Optional*): Use the current value of this phase in amperes. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  * **power** (*Optional*): Use the power value on this phase in watts. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  * **reactive_power** (*Optional*): Use the reactive power value on this phase in VAR. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  * **apparent_power** (*Optional*): Use the apparent power value on this phase in VA. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  * **power_factor** (*Optional*): Use the power factor value on this phase. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  * **phase_angle** (*Optional*): Use the phase angle value on this phase in degrees. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  * **peak_current** (*Optional*): Use the peak current value on this phase in amperes. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  * **harmonic_power** (*Optional*): Use the harmonic power value on this phase. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  * **gain_voltage** (*Optional*, int): Voltage gain value to scale the low voltage AC transformer to household mains feed. Defaults to `7305`.
  
  * **gain_ct** (*Optional*, int): CT clamp calibration value for this phase. Defaults to `27961`.
  
  * **offset_voltage** (*Optional*, int): The voltage offset obtained using the offset calibration feature when current and voltage are 0 Defaults to `0`
  
  * **offset_current** (*Optional*, int): The current offset obtained using the offset calibration feature when current and voltage are 0 Defaults to `0`
  
  * **offset_active_power** (*Optional*, int): The active power offset obtained using the power offset calibration feature when current is 0 and a voltage transformer is plugged in and calibrated. Defaults to `0`
  
  * **offset_reactive_power** (*Optional*, int): The reactive power offset obtained using the power offset calibration feature when current is 0 and a voltage transformer is plugged in and calibrated. Defaults to `0`
  
  * **forward_active_energy** (*Optional*): Use the forward active energy value on this phase in watt-hours. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  * **reverse_active_energy** (*Optional*): Use the reverse active energy value on this phase in watt-hours. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/index.html#config-sensor).
  
  

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

phase_b.docs:
The configuration options for the 2nd phase. Same options as `phase_a`.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

phase_c.docs:
The configuration options for the 3rd phase. Same options as `phase_a`.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

spi_id.docs:
**, [ID](https://esphome.io/guides/configuration-types.html#config-id)**: Manually specify the ID of the [SPI Component](https://esphome.io/components/spi.html#spi) if you want to use multiple SPI buses.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

update_interval.docs:
**, [Time](https://esphome.io/guides/configuration-types.html#config-time)**: The interval to check the sensor. Defaults to `60s`.

*See also: [ATM90E32 Power Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#configuration-variables)*

frequency_status.docs:
Reports status based on frequency thresholds.All options from [Text Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/text_sensor/index.html#config-text-sensor).
  * For `50Hz` systems:
    * `LOW` if frequency ≤ 47Hz
    
    * `HIGH` if frequency ≥ 53Hz
    
    
  
  * For `60Hz` systems:
    * `LOW` if frequency ≤ 57Hz
    
    * `HIGH` if frequency ≥ 63Hz
    
    
  
  If the frequency is within the expected range, the output will be `Normal`.

*See also: [atm90e32 Text Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#text-sensor)*

id.docs:
**, [ID](https://esphome.io/guides/configuration-types.html#config-id)**: The ID of the `atm90e32` sensor defined above. Required if using more than one sensor.

*See also: [atm90e32 Text Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#text-sensor)*

phase_status.docs:
Enables per-phase status conditions:
  * **phase_a** (*Optional*): Phase A status field. All options from [Text Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/text_sensor/index.html#config-text-sensor).Will Report:
    * **Over current**: Detected when current ≥ 65.53 A.
    
    * **Over voltage / Voltage Sag**: Based on the configured `line_frequency`. For example:
      * At `60Hz` (120V expected), sag = <78%, over = >122%.
      
      * At `50Hz` (220V expected), sag = <78%, over = >122%.
      
      
    
    * **Phase Loss**: Indicates if the phase has dropped out.If no condition is met, the sensor reports `Okay`.
    
    
  
  * **phase_b** (*Optional*): Phase B phase status field. Same options as Phase A
  
  * **phase_c** (*Optional*): Phase C phase status field. Same options as Phase A
  
  

*See also: [atm90e32 Text Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#text-sensor)*

phase_a.docs:
Phase A status field. All options from [Text Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/text_sensor/index.html#config-text-sensor).Will Report:
  * **Over current**: Detected when current ≥ 65.53 A.
  
  * **Over voltage / Voltage Sag**: Based on the configured `line_frequency`. For example:
    * At `60Hz` (120V expected), sag = <78%, over = >122%.
    
    * At `50Hz` (220V expected), sag = <78%, over = >122%.
    
    
  
  * **Phase Loss**: Indicates if the phase has dropped out.If no condition is met, the sensor reports `Okay`.
  
  

*See also: [atm90e32 Text Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#text-sensor)*

phase_b.docs:
Phase B phase status field. Same options as Phase A

*See also: [atm90e32 Text Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#text-sensor)*

phase_c.docs:
Phase C phase status field. Same options as Phase A

*See also: [atm90e32 Text Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/atm90e32.html#text-sensor)*
